name: Sierra de Guindo Loop from Sancti-Spíritus
name_original: De Sierra de Guindo a Tenebrón — circular desde Sancti-Spíritus
description: This moderate 51.4-mile (82.6 km) road cycling route from Sancti-Spíritus explores the Sierra de Francia region in Salamanca, Spain.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Sancti-Spíritus navigate a landscape characterized by rolling hills and river valleys, with notable elevation changes. The region features routes that traverse both open countryside and areas near natural features like the Sierra de Guindo. Road cyclists can expect varied terrain, including climbs towards passes and descents through rural areas. Best road cycling routes around Sancti-Spíritus The most popular road cycling route is San Felices Castle and Lumbrales Loop, a 41.0 miles (66.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 58 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of historical sites and rural landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sierra de Guindo Loop from Sancti-Spíritus, a moderate 51.4 miles (82.6 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain through the Sierra de Guindo area. Local road cyclists also love the Lumbrales – Camaces River loop from Villavieja de Yeltes, a 43.3 miles (69.7 km) trail leading through the Camaces River valley, often completed in about 3 hours 2 minutes. Road cycling around Sancti-Spíritus is defined by river valleys, rolling hills, and mountain passes, with options for both moderate and advanced road cycling. The network offers routes for different ability levels, from steady climbs to longer, more challenging distances. The routes in Sancti-Spíritus are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 30 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Sancti-Spíritus's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Sancti-Spíritus?
A: Sancti-Spíritus offers a selection of 5 road cycling routes, catering to various skill levels. These routes navigate through rolling hills and river valleys, providing diverse cycling experiences.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Sancti-Spíritus?
A: While there are no routes specifically graded as 'easy', four of the available routes are classified as 'moderate'. These routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027015425/san-felices-castle-and-lumbrales-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Felices Castle and Lumbrales Loop&lt;/a&gt;, offer a good balance of distance and elevation, making them accessible for cyclists with some experience.

Q: What options are there for advanced road cyclists in Sancti-Spíritus?
A: Advanced road cyclists will find challenging terrain, particularly on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10411906" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morasverdes – Los Lobos Pass (1,499 m) loop from Alba de Yeltes&lt;/a&gt;. This route features significant elevation gain, including a climb to Los Lobos Pass, providing a demanding ride through the region's mountainous areas.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Sancti-Spíritus?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Sancti-Spíritus are designed as loops. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026973264/sierra-de-guindo-loop-from-sancti-spiritus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Guindo Loop from Sancti-Spíritus&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20141373" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lumbrales – Camaces River loop from Villavieja de Yeltes&lt;/a&gt;, allowing you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: What can I see along the road cycling routes in Sancti-Spíritus?
A: The routes offer a mix of natural landscapes and historical sites. You can explore charming settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5455589" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sancti-Spíritus&lt;/a&gt; itself, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4773676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanjuanejo&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5455585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bogajo&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also pass by significant landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4773674" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of La Caridad&lt;/a&gt;, offering cultural points of interest during your ride.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in Sancti-Spíritus?
A: For those seeking longer rides, Sancti-Spíritus has several routes exceeding 40 miles (64 km). The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026973264/sierra-de-guindo-loop-from-sancti-spiritus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Guindo Loop from Sancti-Spíritus&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, covers over 51 miles (82 km) with substantial elevation, making it a great option for endurance cyclists.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Sancti-Spíritus?
A: The region generally experiences pleasant weather for cycling during spring and autumn, with milder temperatures. Summer can be warm, especially in the midday, while winter might bring cooler temperatures and a higher chance of rain, though cycling is possible year-round depending on personal preference and weather conditions.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes in Sancti-Spíritus?
A: The road cycling routes in Sancti-Spíritus are primarily moderate to difficult, featuring rolling hills and significant elevation changes. While some moderate routes might be manageable for active families, there are no routes specifically designated as 'easy' or tailored for very young children. Cyclists should be comfortable with varied terrain and some climbing.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy about the routes in Sancti-Spíritus?
A: The road cycling routes in Sancti-Spíritus are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, which includes both open countryside and challenging climbs through areas like the Sierra de Guindo, as well as the scenic river valleys.

Q: Is parking available for road cyclists in Sancti-Spíritus?
A: While specific parking information for each route start point is not detailed, Sancti-Spíritus and surrounding towns typically offer public parking areas. It's advisable to check local parking options in the starting village of your chosen route, such as Villavieja de Yeltes or Alba de Yeltes.

Q: Are there road cycling routes that pass through or near cafes and refreshment stops?
A: Many of the routes connect or pass through small towns and villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5455589" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sancti-Spíritus&lt;/a&gt;, Lumbrales, or Villavieja de Yeltes. These settlements often provide opportunities to stop for refreshments, local food, or to refill water bottles, especially along routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027015425/san-felices-castle-and-lumbrales-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Felices Castle and Lumbrales Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are permits required for road biking trails in Sancti-Spíritus?
A: Generally, permits are not required for road cycling on public roads and designated routes in the Sancti-Spíritus region. However, always respect private property and any local signage you may encounter, especially when passing through rural or protected areas.
tips: Small municipality that is located in the Salmantina region of Campo de Yeltes, about 80 km southwest of the city of Salamanca, the provincial capital. The origin of Morasverdes can be established around the numerous flour mills that were established along the river of the same name.
Passing through the town of Serradilla del Arroyo, you come to the Parochial Church of San Lorenzo, which is located one side of the road and is tidy and beautiful.

Its name, according to legend, long ago there was a dark forest, in the direction of Tenebrón (hence the name) where there were many wolves, and when someone went to a certain place through this dark forest, they were told God Save Him so that God protect him along the way.

faqs.question: Where is the trail 'From Sierra de Guindo to Tenebrón — circular from Sancti-Spíritus' actually located?
faqs.answer: Despite the name, this road cycling route is located in the province of Salamanca, Spain, specifically within the beautiful Sierra de Francia natural park. The starting point, Sancti-Spíritus, refers to the town in Salamanca, not the city in Cuba.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time for road cycling in the Sierra de Francia region, including this route, is typically from spring through autumn (April to October). During these months, the weather is generally milder and more predictable, offering pleasant conditions for cycling. Winter can bring colder temperatures and potential for adverse weather.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle in the Sierra de Francia Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads and trails within the Sierra de Francia Natural Park. However, always respect local regulations, private property, and any specific rules posted for protected areas.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate in difficulty. With a distance of approximately 82.6 kilometers and an elevation gain of around 717 meters, it requires a reasonable level of fitness and experience with road cycling. Beginners might find the distance and climbs challenging.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car to start this circular route in Sancti-Spíritus?
faqs.answer: Sancti-Spíritus is a small town, and you should be able to find public parking spaces within the town center or near its outskirts. It's advisable to look for parking close to the main roads or public areas where you can safely leave your vehicle for the duration of your ride.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows paved roads, typical for racebike cycling. You can expect undulating hills with some climbs and descents as you traverse the Sierra de Francia landscape. The elevation gain of 717 meters indicates a varied profile, offering both challenging sections and scenic stretches.

faqs.question: What points of interest or towns will I pass through on the way?
faqs.answer: Along this circular route, you will pass through several charming settlements. Notable highlights include Morasverdes, Serradilla del Arroyo, and Dios le Guarde. These towns offer glimpses into local life and potential spots for a break.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with any other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route coincide with other established paths. You will find yourself on parts of the Camino Torres - 01 Salamanca to Almeida, the Cañada Real de Extremadura, the Camino de los Valejones, and the Camino del Mejorito.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is a road cycling route primarily on public roads, dogs are generally not permitted to accompany cyclists on the bike itself for safety reasons. If you are traveling with a dog, it would need to remain off the route or be transported safely in a suitable carrier, which is not typical for racebike cycling.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food, drinks, or accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through several small towns like Sancti-Spíritus, Tenebrón, Morasverdes, and Serradilla del Arroyo, you should find local cafes, bars, or small shops where you can refuel. Accommodation options might be available in the larger towns, but it's always best to check in advance, especially during peak season.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a day of cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: For this moderate road cycling route, essential items include a well-maintained racebike, appropriate cycling attire, a helmet, water bottles (at least 2 liters), energy snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), sun protection, and a fully charged phone. Given the rural nature of some sections, a GPS device or the komoot app with the route downloaded is highly recommended.
